title: Cheapest Flights Within K Stops
problem_statement: Find the minimum cost to travel from a source city to a destination city with at most K stops.

examples.explanation: Take 0->1->2 for cost 200 with one stop in city 1.

intuition: Use BFS-like search by stop count with cost tracking, or relax edges up to K+1 times to respect the stop limit.

approaches.name: Bellman-Ford with stop limit
approaches.description: Relax all edges for up to K+1 rounds, tracking the minimum cost to each city without exceeding the stop limit.
approaches.why_not: Dijkstra alone may violate the stop count; use a modified version or Bellman-Ford to enforce the bound.

interview_qa.a: Each iteration adds one more flight edge, so K stops means K+1 flights maximum.

interview_qa.q: What if a cheaper path uses more stops?
interview_qa.a: It is invalid if it exceeds the allowed stop count, even if the cost is lower.
